perturbation.original_text: As of the VNBJ Closing, except as expressly set forth in this Amendment, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate and all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall read and be interpreted to mean VNBZ and/or ANRA except as the context otherwise requires.
perturbation.changed_text: As of the VNBJ Closing, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate and all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall read and be interpreted to mean VNBZ and/or ANRA except as the context otherwise requires.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except as expressly set forth in this Amendment', Article 1a now provides an unconditional termination of the JV agreement concerning VNBJ upon the VNBJ Closing. However, Article 1d states that the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ shall not release any Veoneer Party or any Nissin Party from liability for breaches that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing. This creates an in-text contradiction because Article 1a seemingly terminates all obligations, but Article 1d carves out an exception for pre-existing liabilities.
perturbation.location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement a.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement d.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the termination of the JV Agreement with respect to VNBJ, VNBZ or the Veoneer Parties shall not release any Veoneer Party or any Nissin Party from liability for the breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or agreements set forth in the JV Agreement that arise prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the JV Agreement would cease with the exception of what's outlined in the amendment. The changed text removes this exception, creating an unconditional termination. However, the contradicted text states that the termination does not release liability for breaches prior to closing. This creates a direct conflict: one clause suggests all obligations are terminated, while the other maintains certain liabilities. Thus, there is a strong in-text contradiction that could lead to confusion and legal disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Except as expressly set forth in this Amendment and notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall cease to be a party to the JV Agreement and shall have no further obligations with respect thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties, unless otherwise agreed in writing and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall cease to be a party to the JV Agreement and shall have no further obligations with respect thereto.
perturbation.explanation: By introducing 'unless otherwise agreed in writing' in Article 1c, the termination of the JV agreement for Veoneer Parties becomes conditional. This contradicts the initial, more definitive statement in Article 1c that the JV agreement shall terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties, which initially suggested a complete and unconditional termination. This contradicts the idea that the Veoneer Parties shall cease to be a party and shall have no further obligations with respect thereto, now it can be conditioned.
perturbation.location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement c.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Article 1.Amendment and Termination of JV Agreement c.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly set forth in this Amendment and notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall cease to be a party to the JV Agreement and shall have no further obligations with respect thereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a conditional clause ('unless otherwise agreed in writing') regarding the termination of the JV Agreement for Veoneer Parties. This directly contradicts the original, more definitive statement, found in the 'Contradicted Text', that the JV Agreement 'shall terminate in its entirety' and that the Veoneer Parties 'shall have no further obligations,' which previously suggested a complete and unconditional termination. The added condition creates ambiguity and potential conflict regarding the enforceability of the termination, leading to an in-text contradiction as described in the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition. Thus, it creates ambiguity where certainty was intended in the initial expression.

perturbation.original_text: Article 4.Effectiveness This Amendment shall only become effective upon the VNBJ Closing with respect to Article 1.1 and the VNBZ Closing with respect to Article 1.2, and shall terminate without any force or effect in the event that the VNBJ SPA and the VNBZ SPA are terminated in accordance with the terms thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 4.Effectiveness This Amendment shall become immediately effective. Termination shall only occur if all parties mutually agree in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The original Article 4 states that the amendment's effectiveness is contingent upon the VNBJ and VNBZ closings and terminates if the corresponding SPAs are terminated. By changing this to 'This Amendment shall become immediately effective. Termination shall only occur if all parties mutually agree in writing,' we introduce a direct contradiction. The amendment now purports to be effective immediately and only terminable by mutual written agreement, conflicting with the original conditional effectiveness and termination clauses tied to the SPA terminations. This directly contradicts the intent and conditions laid out in the original.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Amendment shall only become effective upon the VNBJ Closing with respect to Article 1.1 and the VNBZ Closing with respect to Article 1.2, and shall terminate without any force or effect in the event that the VNBJ SPA and the VNBZ SPA are terminated in accordance with the terms thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction. The original text states the amendment becomes effective upon specific closings and terminates if SPAs are terminated. The changed text makes the amendment immediately effective and terminable only by mutual written agreement. This directly conflicts with the original conditional effectiveness and termination clauses, creating an in-text contradiction that could lead to disputes.
